description: AstroImageJ is open with a VLT V-band image of globular cluster Messier 12 (m12_vband.fits). Your task is to measure the Point Spread Function (PSF) Full Width at Half Maximum (FWHM) to assess image quality and determine the optimal photometry aperture. Measure the FWHM of at least 5 isolated, bright stars outside the crowded core. Calculate the mean FWHM in pixels and convert it to arcseconds using the plate scale of 0.25 arcsec/pixel. Determine the recommended photometry aperture radius (2.5 * mean_FWHM_pixels). Save your results to ~/AstroImages/psf_analysis/fwhm_results.txt following the format specified in ~/AstroImages/psf_analysis/instructions.txt.
